channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Temtem: Pioneers Gets Exclusive Fossil Tems, Crema Previews Solo and Co-op Play & The Variety of Biomes
item.content:encoded:
The Temtem: Pioneers Kickstsrter campaign hit a new stretch goal that will bring all-new Tems to the game that are only available via fossil. Crema also shared info on the social aspects of the game, including the solo and co-op experiences, along with a look at the biomes we’ll see in the Downbelow.

Fossil Tems are coming as a result of hitting a €250,000 goal, with a total of six Tems to discover (three and their evolutions). In order to catch them you will need to find fossil pieces throughout the world using a new tracking system and the help of one Dr. Sasaya,  who can help you turn the fossil pieces into a living creature. 

This will all work via in-game quest and when you complete that, you'll gain access to a new dungeon as well. With meeting this goal, the team actually adjusted future stretch goal amounts to make them more doable, and they've already unlocked an improved mount system at €300k.

The team is also sharing more about the social experience we’ll have in the survival crafting ARPG. You can play solo, with your own personal server instance of the Downbelow offline and local saves (or use Steam Cloud). If you want a more social game, you can play with up to five friends in co-op. 

You can turn your existing personal offline server into an online multiplayer server if you want, But the server will only be accessible as long as the host stays online, so if you want  the rest of the group to have access, you may want to run a dedicated server on another machine or rent something via a server host. 

The team’s latest preview covers Temtem: Pioneers’ biomes. Crema promises a variety of biomes, with some existing within others, and others existing all on their own. The variety means lots of Temtem species to encounter within the various environments. Intentional because the team doesn't want to limit searches for particular species to just one or two small zones.  Because of this, even when there's an overarching theme to a biome, you can expect a variety of types there.

The day/night cycle will be felt all over in every biome, though different biomes may be in daylight while others are in nighttime simultaneously. This means if you are playing co-op with friends, you could be exploring in daylight and your friend in a nighttime biome while playing at the same time. This day / night cycle affects which species will spawn. 

Each biome has Landmarks, significant places within that could be a specific spawn spot, a quest point, a source for a particular resource, or  something else. Accessing some biomes will be tougher than others through the presence of strong bosses.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/temtem-pioneers-gets-exclusive-fossil-tems-crema-previews-solo-and-co-op-play-and-the-variety-of-biomes-2000137874
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hands-Off Preview: Palia's New Expansion Promises A Big Map, Big Horses, and Big Ambitions
item.content:encoded:
The cozy adventure MMORPG Palia has been chugging along with regular updates to grow its wholesome, post-post-apocalyptic world as it pushes through its Early Access development. With the next major content expansion, the first at this scale in nearly a year, it seems Singularity 6 is more ambitious than ever. 

As it touts 10 million players with a new celebration event, it’s also unveiled what’s to come in the upcoming “Royal Highlands” expansion, which brings the titular map and several other features—including a much-awaited mount system that all players can partake in. 

Ahead of the May 12 release, MMORPG was given a “hands-off” demo and showcase of what’s to come in this massive new expansion, with the new map, mount system, and other additions like roving campfires, a dye system, and a fourth floor in estates. In short, it’s a massive undertaking that’ll likely impress fans new and old. 

A Royally Big Map 

Scattered throughout the Royal Highlands are ruins of old civilizations, which players traverse around between cliffs and alongside vast coasts. One specifically gorgeous area turns out to be twice-occupied, twice-abandoned: once by humans before their disappearance, and then once more by other civilizations. 

It’s big. As the developers explain during our demo, a standout mention about the scope of Royal Highlands is that the software engineers had to dig into the game’s map system and fix it in order to allow this new map to fully fit at scale. Even as a “layman” to the game, it’s impressively sizable, both on that map preview and as the developers literally gallop around. This also means the introduction of mounts fits like a glove on a hand—though we’ll get into this later. 

These Royal Highlands have Palia’s players keeping their eyes up for two major gameplay mechanics, an enemy and a farmable threat to the land. Players will want to go around and literally hit up the new “amber crystals,” which contribute to the region’s rewards. These are effectively mineable, but they’re also often found growing (and holding captive) other resources, even all the way down to fishing holes. By clearing the amber crystals before farming these resources, players will receive extra amber crystals as part of the resource farm. 

What’s more of a threat throughout the Royal Highlands are new “masked creatures,” which add a touch of strategy to the game’s combat by modifying behavior and weak points. In short, there are two masks on these creatures: a fire mask and a water one. The fire-masked creatures have a shield in the front and are more likely to charge at you, while water creatures have a shield in the back, requiring a different, more direct approach.

While it appears these "masks" can be applied to many creatures, I can also already tell players will adore the Jackcat, the new hunting creature that players can find around this new region. 

One more feature that I’m sure’ll be most helpful is the new campsite system. There’s one that’s permanent towards the start of Royal Highlands, but players will need to find the others throughout the Highlands in order to take advantage of its new perks. 

At the literal center of these campfires will be the new cooking fire, which, for the first time, will let players literally cook up new items in the middle of seemingly nowhere that’ll restore their focus, as any other food item can. It seems like a simple addition, but given how much else there is to explore around the new map, searching for these each day will absolutely be a sort of effort I imagine the community collaborating on. 

I was also given a preview of a main story cutscene featuring Eshelon—a new Villager who seems to have sway over the legendary Beast of the Elderwood as players approach a special new estate. (Eshelon will also absolutely have sway over the player base. Listen, Singularity 6. Maybe I’ll grind this game out if I can romance him, too.)

Horsing Around 

Definitely one of the more stellar highlights of this demo was the horse, which all players will have access to when they traverse over to Royal Highlands. Fitting for the ruin-themed, horses appear in this region after going missing for ages on ages, much like the humans of Palia. 

Thankfully, the basic horse mount, known as Dawnspur, is available free to all players. However, there are two others available as mounts for players to buy as part of Premium packs for Royal Highlands: the Akwinduu Thoroughbred and the Phoenix Dawnspur, which each have their own splendid looks to them. 

While these are effectively introducing the game’s new “paid mount” system, the important side note is that all are available to breed for free as part of the game’s Ranching system (introduced since the last expansion). In short, you don’t need to pay to earn their respective Ranching drops, including literally fertilizer usable in other systems (made of exactly what you think of), as well as spicy foods from the Phoenixfire Dawnspur. Plus, you can breed different colors of the horses, so you probably aren’t just stuck with the brown default. 

Back to the mount system itself, what they showed of it was rather impressive. In fact, I was told the Royal Highlands themselves were heavily built towards horse traversal, with beautiful little points to jump over. Seeing the leaping and bounding around the map definitely shows how this system was massively built on the imagined aesthetic of what horseriding is, and it’ll be a fun experience to give a go. It even includes four famous “gaits:” walking, the showmanship-like trotting, a “canter,” and the classic gallop. 

If players are really gunning to show off their horseback riding skill, I’m shown that not only are players allowed to bring the horse into Palia’s housing system, but players are actively encouraged to make little racetracks with them. This doesn’t extend to NPC housing nor town centers—though it certainly makes sense to not have everyone racing around in the middle of the city. 

Housing Around (And Upwards)

On the note of housing, this demo also gave a look at the new furniture and the updates to the system, including the new “fourth floor.” 

The fourth floor buildability is available through the Aristocrat furniture pack that’ll be available through the update’s corresponding “Path of the Pony.” As a recap, the “Lunar Paths" are Palia’s “FOMO-free” battle pass that, much like in Helldivers 2, can be accessed even beyond the “season” it was introduced in. For that reason, I’ve been told you’ll always need to unlock it through this one specific Lunar Path. 

It’s still a boon for Palia’s building enthusiasts, if you’re willing to take it up. After adding a staircase from the third floor upwards, you’ll get enough space for a room and a balcony, and it features backwards compatibility with other furniture sets you may have—which, of course, assumedly means that it’ll also be compatible with future sets. And even if you don’t, you still have the free-to-play title’s generous three floors of space to work with. 

The other feature that’ll be a huge boon to Palia players is the new clothing dye system, which is actually quite straightforward. Basically, you buy a dye with Tailor Tickets once, and you have it unlocked forever–-basically, you get to keep your vibe consistent across your entire wardrobe. 

Of course, as always, Palia gets little new bits of nifty furniture and the like. I’m shown a lovely new jade-themed “tint” color scheme for furniture sets that goes stunningly on the Aristocrat pack, given the “royal” vibes, and the horse and jackcat plushies are absolutely going to be a much-desired addition to the home. 

Conclusion 

Overall, given everything launches smoothly on May 17, it feels like this next step in Palia’s major content updates is going to be a boon to its players. 

Here at MMORPG, we’ve been keeping an eye on Palia for a long time, which means we get to see the highs and lows (rarely, knock on wood for a studio of this size) of its progress. It’s still quite clear when you look at what they’re adding each step that this is an “Early Access” game, yet seeing the forest for the trees feels like a treat here. 

Specifically, the new Royal Highlands map feels like not only a fascinating further venture into Palia’s world, but also like a glimpse at what’s truly possible for the relatively wholesome adventure title. I keep thinking about the little technical details that went into making this a true region for the new mount system, not just in building the horse-friendly jumping, but also in creating such a grand and vast map that invokes the spirit of big-map greats like Breath of the Wild or Red Dead. 

And I know it seems really mundane, but as MMORPG’s resident vanilla screenshot enthusiast, the simplified but flexible dye system is going to be a delight. To dig deeper into that feeling, between this and the new campfire system on that massive new map, generally, it seems like Singularity 6 gets that in a “cozy” game, “quality of life” per se is everything. Obviously, you can’t hand a player everything on a silver platter, lest it’s more Second Life or VRChat and less something a player wants to dump their hobby-labor into. But once they’ve earned something, it should at least seem more frictionless.  

Maybe watching them run a horse around the map is making me say it this way—but with Palia, it feels like Singularity 6 is really reaching a stride.

Victoria Rose

Victoria has professionally written about games since 2016, including small former tenures with Polygon and Fanbyte. She mostly spends time in FFXIV, head-deep in roleplay campaigns or stubbornly playing Black Mage through high-end raids. Former obsessions include Dota 2 and The Secret World (also mostly roleplaying). Come visit their estate: Diabolos (Crystal DC), Goblet, Ward 4, Plot 28.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/hands-off-preview-palias-new-expansion-promises-a-big-map-big-horses-and-big-ambitions-2000137870
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: World of Warcraft's Latest Midnight Update, 12.0.5, is Live, Bringing More Void Advances, and New Challenges
item.content:encoded:
Engage in new activities, experience more of the story in Midnight, and earn more loot as the efforts to push back the forces of the Void and their agents continue. You’ll undertake new Void Assaults, disrupt powerful Ritual Sites, get a boost with Voidforge, group up for Decor Duels, and more.

Stave Off Void Assaults

The forces of the Void are vying for position and control amongst themselves in a bid for more power. As they seek every opportunity they can, attacks begin in the Eversong Woods and Zul’Aman zones. [Learn More]

Void Strike

  • Void Strikes take part in Eversong Woods or Zul’Aman on a weekly rotation. Once completed, a new one will begin in another part of the zone and continue to rotate through the zones.

Void Incursion

  • These larger scenarios occur when enough Void Strikes have been completed in the zones and will take the might of many of Azeroth’s champions to defeat them.

Disrupt Ritual Sites

Put a stop to rituals meant to empower the ambitions of power-hungry naga and Twilight’s Blade cultists in small one to five player instances. As you take on greater challenges, you’ll be able to receive greater rewards.

In Ritual Sites, you’ll choose some of the challenges you’ll face as you climb through the tiers. Players will also unlock Great Vault rewards for the World content row alongside Delves and Prey.

Players will earn Field Accolades from both Void Assaults and Ritual Sites as currency they can use to purchase Champion and Heroic quality gear and rewards. [Learn More]

Get a Leg Up With the Voidforge

Voidforge will provide new opportunities for players in Midnight to take their game further. You’ll help aid domanaar Decimus build a Voidforge to begin transmuting Nebulous Voidcores, which are transmutable into powerful equipment upon defeating powerful foes in Midnight Season 1 raids or completing Mythic+ dungeons, and Bountiful Delves and Prey Hunts on Nightmare difficulty.

After unlocking the Voidforge, Decimus will require additional assistance to craft the Ascendant Nilhammer, allowing players to upgrade their weapons and trinkets to new heights by collecting Ascendant Voidcores. [Learn More]

Hide or Seek With Decor Duels

Amidst all the danger that swirls above Azeroth, there’s something to be said about having a little fun. Players can join in a team vs team hide-and-seek style game taking place in Silvermoon City through the Group Finder Tool (PvP tab) to either hide as various Housing decor items or seek out those who have donned the guise of an item. You’ll earn new rewards, including a mount, toys, and Housing decor, through this bite-sized event. [Learn More]

New Activity: Abyss Anglers

Dive deep into the shimmering waters off Zul'Aman's rugged coast, where schools of exotic fish swarm just beneath the surface in a new repeatable event—Abyss Anglers. Team up to spear as many as you can and rack up points to unlock fishing rewards and upgrades to your diver gear, allowing you to venture deeper into the abyss where the greatest treasures await.

Seek out Depthdiver Jeju to suit up and enter the Depths. Zip through whirlpools, catch creatures with your harpoon, and discover treasures amidst the underwater steam vents.

Prove your mastery to earn dive equipment upgrades and cosmetics.

New Mythic+ Dungeon Feature and Achievements

New Feature Lindormi's Glow

Lindormi's Glow highlights select enemy forces even when the Lindormi's Guidance affix is inactive. Defeating all highlighted enemies will complete the enemy forces requirement for a Keystone Dungeon.

  • Any player whose class supports a tank role specialization can choose to use Lindormi's Glow by speaking with Lindormi near the The Timeways portal or at the end of a Keystone Dungeon.
  • Lindormi's Glow will activate in a Keystone Dungeon if the party's tank has chosen to use this feature.
  • Players can disable Lindormi's Glow by speaking with Lindormi again.

New Achievements

Midnight Keystone Myth: Season One – Attain a Mythic+ Rating of at least 3400 during Midnight Season 1. Rewards a Timelost Saddle that can be exchanged with Lindormi for a mount of your choice. Mounts include: A variety of new mounts, and Keystone Master and Keystone Legend mounts from previous seasons.

Umbral Champion: Midnight Season One – End Midnight Mythic+ Season 1 with a Mythic+ Rating in the top 1% of all players in your region. Rewards a new exclusive mount.

12.0.5 Content Notes

Learn more about all the changes in our content update notes.

More adventures are calling you home.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/world-of-warcrafts-latest-midnight-update-1205-is-live-bringing-more-void-advances-and-new-challenges-2000137873
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
